narration: Sort Colors is simpler than general sorting because there are only three possible values.
callout: That small set of possible values makes counting a great strategy.

narration: After counting the 0s, 1s, and 2s, we rewrite the array in that order.
decision: Count first, then rebuild the array.

predictionPrompt.prompt: Why is counting such a good fit here?

choices.label: Because there are only three possible values
choices.feedback: Correct. Counting each kind is enough.

choices.label: Because the values are random
choices.feedback: Not this one. The key fact is that the values come from only three choices.

narration: The special structure of the data changes what sorting method makes sense.
callout: That is an important problem-solving habit.
